title: T-045 — Re-run Confirmation Modal
prompt:
# T-045 — Re-run Confirmation Modal

PRD: `docs/prd/07-PLANNING-EXT-PRD.md` §17.1 PLE-009, §16.2 OQ-EXT-09, R14.
UX/prototype refs are listed in `pipeline_inputs.scope_files`.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Add the safe re-run confirmation step used from run history and run detail.

## Implementation contract
Clone only allowed input params/snapshot references; enqueue via the same T-012 path; existing run and approved schedule remain unchanged until new assignments are approved.

ACP shape reminder: keep this task as one atomic implementation. Do not add ACP-owned fields. Keep local dependencies in `dependencies`; cross-module contracts in `cross_module_dependencies` are blockers/inputs, not ACP local task IDs.

pipeline_inputs.description: Implement confirmation dialog for cloning a previous scheduler run into a new UUID v7 run request.
pipeline_inputs.details: Matches rerun_confirm_modal. Server Action clones safe input params and always creates a new run_id to avoid idempotency collision.
acceptance_criteria: Given a source run, then modal summary shows source run/horizon/lines/include_forecast and prototype parity.
Given Confirm is clicked, then server generates a new UUID v7 and never reuses source run_id.
Given source run is failed/cancelled or user lacks Planner role, then action returns 403/409 and no run is queued.
test_strategy: RED action tests for new UUID, RBAC, failed-run block; RTL modal snapshot.
Run pnpm --filter web test scheduler/RerunConfirmModal rerun-action.
risk_red_lines: Do not create idempotency collision by reusing source run_id.
Do not expose rerun to read-only roles.
